question: A website tracks average sign-ups per year. According to the chart, which of the following statements is true?

options.A: The latest year shows a sharp decline compared to previous years.
options.B: The latest year proves that sign-ups are now permanently declining.
options.C: The chart only shows sign-ups up to part of the latest year, so it does not support conclusions about the full-year trend.
options.D: The latest year has the lowest full-year sign-ups in the entire period.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 3 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (333 sign-ups) directly to full-year values like 1,304 sign-ups creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
